title: Apply clock gating to idle logic
description: Gate clocks to registers that hold their value so they stop toggling, using glitch-free gating cells.
body: Use when a block has registers that retain state across many idle cycles. Do not hand-code a clock gate with combinational logic, since that risks glitches on the clock. Actions: identify idle register banks, insert a glitch-free integrated clock-gating cell, and verify the enable logic in simulation. Validation signals include reduced dynamic power in the gated region and a clean gated clock with no glitches in the waveform. Risk boundary: a glitchy gate can clock garbage into registers, so deny ad hoc combinational gating and use the library gating cell.
